readouts_added_this_turn.9_dHfuzz_dlambda: near-zero for Ne/Si (fixed branch struct); no split
readouts_added_this_turn.10_fuzz_tree_entropy_level: {Ne,Si} high {Se,Ni} low = Axis-1 (branch count = channel rank)

structural_diagnosis: Kraus branch count = dissipator rank (Ne=4,Se=3,Ni=2,Si=2), a DYNAMICS/Axis-1 property. Any fuzz measure built from Kraus branches inherits Axis-1. Axis-0 (intuition/sensing) is not about HOW MANY futures a channel spawns.
refined_hypothesis: Axis-0 'tense' (future-possibility vs present-actuality orientation) is a property of the COMPOSED ENGINE LOOP (deductive UEUE vs inductive EUEU), not of a single terrain. A terrain in isolation has no tense. At loop level, 3/4 families separate correctly; Ni fails only because it is a source-locked pure attractor that closes distinguishability regardless of tense.
best_result: engine-loop tense: 3/4 correct sign pattern (Ne+ Se- Si-), highest yet
matches_owner_doctrine: Axis-0 is a LATE object downstream of loop/engine structure (teeth map spine), NOT terrain-local. Confirmed from 11 failed/partial terrain-and-fuzz-local readouts.
concrete_next: Test Axis-0 at the FULL 720-degree double-loop level (2x360, outer/inner D/I swap) with Ni handled as the pure-attractor special case; or build it as a genuinely downstream rho_AB->Phi_0 readout after the loop, not before.
